title: Retries, Idempotency, Compensation, and Recovery · Recovery Bay
summary: Harden a loop that calls unreliable external services. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a fault-injected recovery harness and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use bounded retries with backoff and error classification.
Design idempotent operations and deduplication for repeated requests.
Checkpoint durable state so a process can restart without replaying completed effects.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""Bounded agent-loop portfolio. No external effects occur in this browser lab."""

steps.title: Order the regression proof
steps.instruction: Order the Recovery Tests evidence cycle before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Recovery Tests assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Inject timeouts, partial failures, duplicates, and restarts into loop tests. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Compare plausible approaches against the decision, constraints, evidence quality, and cost of being wrong.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Use a changed worked example case derived from this module: loops retries idempotency. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result. A defensible solution applies Recovery Tests, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a fault-injected recovery harness.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Happy-path integration tests cover distributed failure behavior. This fails the Recovery Tests contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
